message:
fix(messaging): clean up NATS subscription on SUB write failure

In subscribe() and queue_subscribe(), the subscription state was
inserted into the shared subscriptions map BEFORE sending the SUB
command to the server. If write_all or flush failed, the subscription
entry leaked — holding a channel sender that would accumulate dead
sends on every matching incoming message.

Fix: remove the subscription entry from the map before returning
the error, in both write_all and flush error paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
